role: Host-side resolver consumer that builds a modal allocation AST from the current device coordinate, posts it to /api/resolve, and writes the returned values to CSS custom properties on :root.
capabilities: Build modal allocation invariant AST using fork resolver grammar (field, lit, mul, sub, clamp, select)
Derive live device coordinate and orientation from window.innerWidth/innerHeight
POST invariant + coordinate to /api/resolve and parse the resolved slot response
Apply resolved numeric slots as CSS custom properties with px units where required
Degrade-clean fallback that leaves CSS vars unset on any failure so body[data-ff] CSS fallbacks hold
Monotonic sequence guard to prevent stale async resolves from overwriting newer results
Primitive-value guard to avoid writing non-primitive resolved values into CSS
substance_why: It implements the full pipeline from coordinate capture and AST authoring through network resolution, concurrency guard, and CSS application, with documented degrade-clean behavior.
interactions_present: none — only exported functions (coordinateFromWindow, resolveAndApplyModal) for a caller to invoke
interactions_missing: No resize/orientationchange event listeners are registered in this file
No debouncing or throttling of coordinate changes beyond the sequence guard
No loading or pending state surfaced to the user during resolve
No user-visible error or degraded-state feedback when /api/resolve fails
No cancellation of in-flight fetch requests superseded by newer coordinates
performance_risks: Unbounded /api/resolve POSTs if called on every resize/orientationchange without caller-side debounce
Stale in-flight fetches continue consuming network/resources even after newer coordinates arrive
Repeated window.innerWidth/innerHeight reads and CSS custom property writes may force layout recalc during resize storms
Network latency can cause resolved CSS values to lag behind the live viewport
reuse_why: Keep the resolver-client contract, degrade-clean guards, and sequence pattern, but generalize the modal-specific invariant and CSS var mapping for reuse across surfaces.
decomposition_hints: Split generic resolver client (coordinate capture, /api/resolve call, sequence guard, value validation) from modal-specific invariant and slot mapping
Extract AST builder primitives into a shared resolver-DSL module
Move CSS custom-property name mapping and unit handling into a layout tokens/theme layer
Separate device coordinate detection into a viewport/orientation utility module
